label: Authenticate
description: Verify that a user or organisation is who they claim to be, using trusted credentials, before securely granting access to a service.
use_when: Access to a service must be limited to known users or organisations.
The service handles personal, sensitive, or protected information.
Actions or content must be linked to a specific user account.
Legal, policy, or security requirements require identity verification before access.
do_not_use_when: The service is low-risk and can use one-time access links or token-based access.
Guest or anonymous access is appropriate.
Trusted third-party authentication handles the full flow externally.

steps.label: Present sign-in options
steps.description: Show options: direct credentials (username/password) or external identity provider redirect.

steps.label: Verify credentials
steps.description: Verify credentials using the service's authentication system or trusted external provider. Do not expose service data until verification succeeds.

steps.label: Explain failure safely
steps.description: Explain failure in a clear, non-technical manner without exposing sensitive security details.

steps.label: Support credential recovery
steps.description: Provide recovery path for forgotten, expired, or invalid credentials. Support reset or referral to support channels.

ux_considerations.text: Support both individual users and organisations where relevant.
Account for assisted and offline channels that rely on the same authentication rules.

technical_notes.text: Authentication mechanisms must use trusted and secure credential stores.
Integrations with external identity providers must use secure, standard protocols (e.g. OpenID Connect).
Authentication outcomes must be logged for audit and security purposes.
Session handling must prevent unauthorised reuse or access.
